title: Origanum valentinum
intro: Origanum valentinum, also known as Bulgarian or Valchanov's oregano, is a species of flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to the Balkan Peninsula. The plant has been traditionally used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of indigestion, nausea, and bloating, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Origanum valentinum include carvacrol, thymol, and rosmarinic acid, which contribute to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Origanum valentinum include tea, essential oil, and tincture, which can be used to soothe digestive issues and support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Origanum valentinum is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. The Lamiaceae family consists of herbs, shrubs, and trees commonly known as mint or deadnettles, with fragrant leaves and flowers. This family includes many other species of ornamental and culinary plants, such as oregano, thyme, rosemary, and sage. Origanum valentinum is native to the Mediterranean region and is often used in traditional medicine and as a flavoring agent. Its classification makes it a member of the Lamiaceae family and the genus Origanum, which includes other oregano species.
